target.name: Spaceium
target.founder: Ashi Dissanayake (CEO, University of Ottawa; orbital mechanics + propulsion; NASA aerodynamics + asteroid return-mission simulations), Reza Fetanat (CTO, University of Ottawa; mechanical, cryogenics; revitalized the LR-101 engine used on Atlas/Thor/Delta)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Spaceium is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by orbital mechanics and propulsion specialists from the University of Ottawa, with combined experience in NASA aerodynamics and legacy engine systems. The company has raised $6.3M to date. The Space & Aerospace sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX and Sierra Space, though the narrow seed-stage peer set lacks sufficient datapoints for reliable median comparison.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable valuation method. Using total capital raised of $6.3M as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $15.75M. Peer-median triangulation is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace sector. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, though the sector median trades at 25.8x sales.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13.4M (low) to $18.1M (high), with a median point of $16M. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct seed-stage peer data within the narrow sector, lack of revenue disclosure, and stage-progression methodology's reliance on market-standard multipliers rather than company-specific metrics. Revenue traction and technical de-risking will be critical to validate this range at subsequent funding rounds.
